summary.intent: Run an automated Greptile code review loop (Greploop) on PR #675 fixing channel startup behavior on vault unseal, then address all flagged issues.
summary.outcome: Greptile review completed with a 4/5 score; the P1 bug (missing `continue` causing unsupported channels to fall through to `start_account`) was fixed, all 9 tests passed, and the fix was committed and pushed.

repo: PR #675 is on branch `fix/start-channels-on-vault-unseal` in a worktree at /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/cstewart-hc/fix/start-channels-on-vault-unseal
Greptile is integrated as a CI check on PRs and can be triggered via the GitHub API; its score and issues appear in PR issue comments
The vault unseal flow iterates over channels and must `continue` on unsupported channel types to prevent fall-through to `start_account`

code.finding: Missing `continue` statement caused unsupported channel types to fall through to `start_account` — a P1 logic bug introduced in this PR. Fix: add `continue` after handling the unsupported-channel case.

workflow: Greploop requires an existing open PR; it cannot run on a branch without one
Greptile check output (title/summary fields) may contain the numeric score; issue comments are the reliable fallback source
jq parse errors can occur when polling raw GitHub API check-run responses — fetching status directly is a reliable workaround
P2 Greptile suggestions (tracing::instrument, debug!) may already be addressed in current code; always read the file before acting on review comments

friction: Initial `/greploop` invocation failed because no PR existed for the current branch — required identifying the correct PR number (675) manually
Polling for Greptile check completion hit a jq parse error, requiring a fallback direct status fetch
Greptile score was not in the check-run output fields and required a separate search of PR issue comments
